name: FREEDOM GROUP
location: Gadsden Street United Methodist Church, Enter thru kitchen on left
notes:
Open meetings are available to anyone interested in the AA program of recovery from alcoholism. Non-alcoholics may attend open meetings as observers.
A well-established AA meeting once weekly, Freedom Meeting consists of a community fellowship of long-term members, members with a few years, and new members, beginning their road to serenity through the program of Alcoholics Anonymous. If you would just like to sit in and observe how this program works, speak with members one-on-one, or just enjoy one hour of education in living a new life in peace - we welcome all to our fellowship.

name: Freethinkers
notes:
Inclusivity Statement

Newcomers are approaching AA at the rate often of thousands yearly. They represent almost every belief and attitude imaginable. We have atheists and agnostics. We have people of nearly every race, culture, and religion. In AA we are supposed to be bound together in the kinship of a common suffering. Consequently, the full individual liberty to practice any creed or principle or therapy whatever should be a first consideration for us all. Let us not, therefore, pressure anyone with our individual or even our collective views. Let us instead accord each other the respect and love that is due to every human being as they try to make their way toward the light. Let us always try to be inclusive rather than exclusive; let us remember that each alcoholic among us is a member of AA, so long as they declare.

~ Bill W. Grapevine, July 1965
We meet in the porch during cooler months and in the building during the summer heat (look for signage).
